sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 4-year-old's mother who injured her right knee while skiing in Vermont last weekend.
She fell and her knee popped, and it has been painful ever since.
She was unable to bear weight and had to be taken down by the ski patrol.
The pain is on the outside of the knee and feels like it won't hold her weight.
She was able to bend it immediately after the injury but not now, and straightening it hurts.
She does not have numbness or tingling in her toes.
She is generally healthy, with no high blood pressure or diabetes.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: I prescribed ibuprofen 600-800 mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The heart sounds are in a nice regular rate and rhythm, and there is no murmur.
The lungs are clear to auscultation.

There is edema and an effusion over the right knee, pain to palpation of the right lateral knee, decreased flexion and extension, a positive Lachman sign, palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse, and no leg edema in the ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in her right knee.
Problem list: ACL tear; knee pain; decreased knee mobility.
Plan:
I ordered a knee MRI to confirm the diagnosis and assess for any other potential injuries.
I recommended continued use of ibuprofen 600-800 mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
I prescribed a knee brace to provide support for the knee.
I scheduled a follow-up appointment to discuss the MRI results and further management.
Follow-up: I will see the patient again soon to discuss the MRI results and plan further management.
